steps.title: Find the greatest common factor
steps.content:
In $9x^6 - 63x^2$, identify what both terms share:
  • Coefficients: GCF of 9 and 63 is 9
  • Variables: GCF of $x^6$ and $x^2$ is $x^2$
The overall GCF is $9x^2$.

steps.title: Factor out the GCF
steps.content:
Factor out $9x^2$ from the original expression:

$$9x^6 - 63x^2 = 9x^2(x^4 - 7)$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
